shared.jobContextReminder: Proposals and chats stay tied to the job—they keep scope and hires traceable.
shared.clientLiquidityIntro: Clear briefs attract better replies: say outcome, deadlines, constraints, and how you’ll decide.
shared.freelancerLiquidityIntro: Strong proposals cite the brief, propose a milestone, and show you understood the constraints.

client.checklistIntro: Four real milestones—tracked from your profile, listings, proposals, and messages (not vanity metrics).
client.allComplete: Starter checklist complete. Keep replies fast on proposals—they stay tied to each job listing.

profile.label: Complete client profile
profile.hint: Add company basics and location in Settings—freelancers use this to trust your brief.

first_job.label: Post your first job
first_job.hint: Publish a concise brief so freelancers can bid with realistic scope and timing.

review_proposal.label: Review a proposal
review_proposal.hint: Open Client jobs and inspect bids—compare fit, timeline, and price side by side.

discussion.label: Start a discussion
discussion.hint: Reply from Messages or proposal review—everything stays threaded to the job.

liquidity.b1: Lead with the outcome you need, deliverables, and how you’ll measure “done”.
liquidity.b2: State budget honestly and when you plan to decide—reduces churn for everyone.
liquidity.b3: Respond in Messages for clarifications—the thread stays anchored to that job listing.

freelancer.checklistIntro: Track real progress—profile completeness, catalogue engagement, bids, and first messages.
freelancer.allComplete: Starter checklist complete. Keep proposals scoped to each job—they and chat stay tied to that listing.

profile.label: Complete freelancer profile
profile.hint: Headline, bio, and availability—clients skim this before trusting your bid.

browse_jobs.label: Browse or shortlist roles
browse_jobs.hint: Use Jobs (save listings you like) until you submit your first proposal—counts when you engage with real postings.

first_proposal.label: Send your first proposal
first_proposal.hint: Bid with a concise plan, price, and timeline tied to that job—not a generic pitch.

discussion.label: Open a discussion
discussion.hint: Message from job or proposal flows so context stays tied to the hire.

liquidity.b1: Mirror language from the brief and call out assumptions you validated.
liquidity.b2: Offer a phased plan with one clear next step the client can say yes to.
liquidity.b3: Use Messages for follow-ups—all conversation stays anchored to that job listing.

notifications.pageSubtitle: Bids, messages, verification, and hiring updates—unread stays highlighted until you open it.
notifications.emptyDescription: This is quiet because nothing landed in your inbox. When freelancers bid, conversations start, or status changes arrive, threads show here with quick links.
notifications.filterEmptyDescription: Switch category or wait for new bids, chats, or contract updates.
notifications.unreadCaption: Tap to mark read and jump to related job or conversation when linked.
notifications.readCaption: Opened or dismissed—still here for reference.

messages.pageSubtitle: Inbox + chat tied to hiring—threads stay anchored to roles or contracts when relevant.
messages.emptyThreadsDescription: This is empty because nobody has messaged yet. Threads open from proposals, hired work, or when you tap Message on profiles—everything stays anchored to each job listing.
messages.pickThreadDescription: Pick a thread to read sent messages and reply. On mobile tap a conversation; use Back to return to this inbox.
messages.startThreadBody: Send an opening note to align on scope, timing, or next steps. Messages deliver instantly to the other party.
messages.startBullet1: Clarify scope and decision timeline early.
messages.startBullet2: Reference the job or contract—context carries through automatically.
messages.composeHint: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for a new line
messages.threadTiedHint: Keep updates tied to this job—the client compares proposals in hiring context.
messages.handoffFromProposal: Conversation opened from your latest proposal.
messages.handoffFromConversation: Conversation opened from job proposal review. Continue the discussion and choose your next step.
messages.contextProposalPending: Proposal state is still linking—refresh if this looks out of date.
messages.nextSuggestedDefault: Keep messages about this job’s scope, timeline, and decision so both sides stay aligned.
messages.nextSuggestedClientInvite: No proposal on record yet—you can still leave a concise note referencing the listing.
messages.nextSuggestedClientFirstReply: You have a proposal to review—send a focused reply referencing the brief.
messages.nextSuggestedClientAwaitingFreelancer: You replied—give them a brief window to respond in this same thread.
messages.nextSuggestedClientCompare: You’re messaging with an active applicant—compare price, timeline, and fit against the listing.
messages.nextSuggestedClientShortlisted: You shortlisted them—confirm scope and decision timing before accepting.
messages.nextSuggestedClientAccepted: Accepted—coordinate delivery milestones here so context stays tied to the job.
messages.nextSuggestedClientStale: This proposal ended—stay professional; start fresh on active listings if hiring continues.
messages.nextSuggestedFreelancerDiscover: No bid found on this pairing yet—browse the listing before sending long messages.
messages.nextSuggestedFreelancerAwaitReview: Your proposal is in review—avoid casual chat until the client opens the conversation.
messages.nextSuggestedFreelancerReply: The client messaged—answer with specifics tied to the posted brief.
messages.nextSuggestedFreelancerClarify: Discussion is live—summarize commitments in writing so hiring stays accountable.
messages.nextSuggestedFreelancerAccepted: You’re hired for this listing—drive next steps inside this hiring thread.
messages.composeWorkplaceReminder: This workspace is for job-related coordination—avoid off-topic introductions.

client.subtitleHasProfile: {{workspace}} · track listings, proposals, and hires in one workspace.
client.subtitleNoProfile: Create a client profile to publish roles freelancers can evaluate against.
client.finishProfileCardBody: A few details unlock job posting and help freelancers trust your briefs.
client.jobsEmptyNoProfileBody: What: this list only shows roles you have posted. Why: your profile is not finished for hiring. Next: open Settings, add company details, then post a brief.
client.jobsEmptyFirstBody: What: you have no active listings here. Why: early teams often draft before publishing. Next: publish a clear brief—budget, work mode, and timing—so proposals stay tied to that job.
client.bidsEmptyNoProfileTitle: Bids will appear after you post
client.bidsEmptyNoProfileBody: What: proposal inbox is empty. Why: clients need a live role to receive bids. Next: finish setup and publish at least one job.
client.bidsEmptyNoBidsBody: What: no freelancers have bid on your listings. Why: the board may be quiet or the brief needs more clarity. Next: refine the job, share context, and reply quickly in Messages—threads stay tied to each job.
client.contractsEmptyBody: What: no active hire records. Why: contracts open after you accept a bid. Next: review proposals on your job, shortlist, then accept when ready.
client.summarySub: Counts from live hiring activity—not estimates.
client.statUnreadThreadsHint: You have threads where freelancers are waiting—reply in Messages.
client.statUnreadThreadsClear: No discussion threads are waiting on your reply right now.
client.statUnreadThreadsNoProfileHint: Post a job so proposals and threaded messages can appear here.
client.statOpenJobsHintEmpty: Post a job to open a listing
client.statOpenJobsHintNoProfile: Create a client profile to post
client.statActiveContractsHintEmpty: Accept a bid to open a hire
client.quickActionsSub: High-impact moves for your hiring funnel.
client.contractsSub: Contracts and outcomes tied to your account.

freelancer.profileCardBodyNew: Skills, work mode, and a short bio unlock proposals and full quota visibility.
freelancer.profileCardBodyBoost: {{percent}}% complete—headline, bio, and rates help you win work.
freelancer.activityEmptyNoProfileBody: What: no hiring activity yet. Why: proposals require a complete profile. Next: add bio, headline, and work mode, then send a focused bid from a real job.
freelancer.activityEmptyNoActivityBody: What: no bids or hire updates in your feed. Why: you have not sent a proposal or nothing changed recently. Next: browse open jobs, save roles you like, and send a tailored proposal—chats stay tied to that job.
freelancer.openJobsEmptyBody: What: this panel shows a small sample. Why: filters or timing may hide matches. Next: open the full job board, save roles, and send proposals that reference each brief.
freelancer.subtitleHasProfile: {{name}} · proposals, quota, and open roles in one view.
freelancer.subtitleNoProfile: Finish setup to bid on jobs and track proposals and contracts here.
freelancer.overviewSubtitle: Live counts from your plan and profile.
freelancer.statAwaitingRepliesHintActive: Reply in Messages—the client is waiting on you.
freelancer.statAwaitingRepliesHintClear: No pending replies flagged on your inbox right now.
freelancer.statAwaitingRepliesHintNoProfile: Complete setup so threaded replies can surface here.
freelancer.statBidQuotaHintNoProfile: Create a profile to see plan limits.
freelancer.statQuotaUnlimitedHint: No active bid cap on your current plan.
freelancer.statBidQuotaHintPlan: {{remaining}} of {{limit}} bids remaining on your plan.
freelancer.statProfileHintIncomplete: Add details clients ask for next.
freelancer.nextActionAwaitingBanner: Reply while context is fresh—clients wait on clear updates.
freelancer.heroMotivation: Clients skim profiles fast—lead with relevance, clear timelines, and calm follow‑through.
freelancer.heroStatProposalsHintSetup: Create a profile to start sending proposals tied to briefs.
freelancer.heroStatProposalsHintQuiet: First proposals feel slow—browse open roles today.
freelancer.heroStatContractsHintZero: Accept a bid moves work into an active engagement.
freelancer.heroStatReviewsEmpty: Stars appear once clients finish work together—keep shipping great outcomes.
freelancer.heroStatSavedEmptyHint: When clients bookmark you, it surfaces here.
freelancer.playbookIntro: What makes NearWork chats move: structure, specificity, and client-first language.
freelancer.playbookFooter: Every thread stays tied to the job—reference the brief, keep updates short, and confirm next steps.
freelancer.playbookS1Body: Open with how you read the problem and one relevant proof point—skip generic intros.
freelancer.playbookS2Body: Outline milestones, what you need from the client, and how you will de-risk the first week.
freelancer.playbookS3Body: Mirror the job language, cite similar work, and show you understand constraints.
freelancer.playbookS4Body: Price to what you can deliver well—clear assumptions beat surprise change orders.
freelancer.snapshotSubtitle: Numbers from your NearWork account—no synthetic trends.
freelancer.conversationsEmptyBody: Job discussions open after you send a proposal or get invited—everything stays in Messages.
freelancer.skillsSubtitle: Bars reflect years you added on your profile.
freelancer.skillsEmptyBody: Add skills in your profile so clients can match you faster.

footer.tagline: Freelance marketplace for local and remote work.

hero.title: Find ready-to-work freelancers in minutes
hero.subtitle: Search by skill and city, review real profiles, and hire without scattered chats.
hero.outcomeLine: Search, compare, and start discussions in one workflow - nearby or remote.
hero.marketplaceLiveLine: Live marketplace - search, compare, and start in minutes.
hero.supportingLine: All bids and conversations stay tied to the job.
hero.locationNote: Use city when you need a local freelancer, or leave it empty for remote freelancer results.
hero.howStep1: Post a job or browse freelancers.
hero.howStep3: Chat and hire in one place.
hero.howFootnote: No sign-in required to search—create an account when you are ready to post or send proposals.
hero.searchSubtitle: Real listings · updates with the community
hero.keywordPlaceholder: Try: event photographer, video editor, math tutor
hero.cityHint: Add a city to bias toward nearby profiles; leave it empty for broader remote results.
hero.liveUrgencyLine: New freelancers and active jobs are added daily.
hero.searchTrustLine: Proposals, chats, and decisions stay tied to the job.
hero.searchTrustOne: Proposals stay linked to listing context.
hero.searchTrustTwo: You can compare options before deciding.
hero.proofFreelancersActive: {{count}} active freelancers ready for job-linked proposals
hero.proofBriefsToday: {{count}} structured briefs added in the last 24h
hero.marketplaceActivityLine: {{freelancers}} freelancers active now · New briefs arrive daily · Proposals stay tied to the job
hero.marketplaceActivityFallback: Freelancers stay active and new briefs keep arriving each day.

panel.lineThree: Proposals stay tied to each job
panel.noLiveData: Freelancer availability and new briefs will appear here.

categories.title: Hire freelancers by category
categories.subtitle: Pick a service lane, then narrow by city or mode to start faster.
categories.secondaryCta: Need active briefs? Open job board ->

preview.title: What hiring data looks like
preview.marketSubtitle: Active freelancers and recent jobs you can open right now.

why.popularChoice: Why choose this: popular choice with active demand.
why.recentlyHired: Why choose this: recently hired in similar work.
why.topRatedLocal: Why choose this: top rated in nearby market.

useCases.title: How teams use NearWork
useCases.subtitle: Use nearby for local work, remote for async work, or hybrid when both matter.

onsite.title: Event photographer in your city
onsite.body: Use city and nearby signals to shortlist freelancers who can show up.

remote.title: Video editor for weekly content
remote.body: Compare bids on turnaround, revisions, and scope before hiring.

hybrid.title: Tutor with local and online sessions
hybrid.body: Set location and schedule expectations from the first brief.

finalCta.title: Start with one brief - find talent, discuss, and hire with clarity.
finalCta.subtitle: Keep proposals, decisions, and chats in one workflow from first brief to final hire.
finalCta.urgency: Post once, compare clearly, and move faster.

three.title: Math tutor (hybrid sessions)
three.subtitle: Mix of online and in-person meetings

workspace.label: Workspace
workspace.communityCardBody: We are early access: feedback and help content keep hires tied to real jobs—billing stays transparent when it launches.

jobs.heroVisualBody: Briefs stay tied to real budgets and locations—scan fast, then open what fits your next sprint.
jobs.searchExamplesHint: Try: Figma, UI/UX, React, copywriting, video…
jobs.boardDescription: Use search, filters, and marketplace signals to shortlist roles worth your next proposal—not generic listings.
jobs.pageDescription: Active client briefs in a structured board—filter by category, place, and work mode, then open a row for full scope.
jobs.locationHint: Matches when the job lists a city.
jobs.resultHint: Title + budget at a glance—open for the full brief.
jobs.sidebarBody: Post a brief with budget and location so the right people can send proposals.
jobs.matchForYouHint: Based on listing freshness, budget, or a featured boost on NearWork—not an automated score.
jobs.proposalsMetaHint: Submitted proposals on this job (updates as freelancers apply).
jobs.signalHint: Heuristic cue from this listing (not a guarantee).
jobs.pulseSubtitle: Recent real activity from open roles and proposals.
jobs.pulseEmptyJobs: When new roles publish, they will appear here.
jobs.pulseEmptyProposals: Proposal activity will show as freelancers respond to open briefs.
jobs.notifyCardBody: Turn on notifications to catch fresh roles without constantly refreshing this page.
jobs.insightFootnote: Totals reflect the live database and update as the marketplace moves.
jobs.applyConfidenceLine: Review the brief first. Sending a proposal starts a job-linked conversation, not a commitment.
jobs.quickTipApply: Applying to your first 3 relevant jobs increases visibility faster.
jobs.smallCtaTitle: Still not finding the right job?
jobs.emptyCategoryTitle: No jobs yet in this category
jobs.emptyCategoryBody: Nothing open matches that category right now. The board only shows what clients have actually posted.
jobs.emptyCategoryStep1: Clear category and scan the full board.
jobs.emptyCategoryStep2: Loosen keyword or city if you stacked filters.
jobs.emptyCategoryStep3: Post a brief if you are the one hiring.
jobs.emptyFiltersBody: Early access boards stay sparse—filters are doing their job even when the answer is zero.
jobs.emptyFiltersStep1: Reset work mode to Any, then narrow again.
jobs.emptyFiltersStep2: Drop city if the role might be remote.
jobs.emptyFiltersStep3: Use one keyword from the job title you remember.
jobs.emptyDefaultTitle: No open jobs on the board
jobs.emptyDefaultBody: The public job list is empty until clients post real briefs—there is no demo data behind this view.
jobs.emptyDefaultStep1: Clients: post the first brief with budget and work mode.
jobs.emptyDefaultStep2: Freelancers: browse the directory and polish your profile while jobs ramp up.
jobs.emptyDefaultStep3: Everyone: early access means the feed will move—bookmark and check back.
jobs.emptyActionTitle: Start by posting your first job
jobs.emptyActionBody: There are no jobs on the board yet. Post a clear brief first, then freelancers can review and send proposals in context.
jobs.emptyRoleClient: As a client, posting your first brief is the fastest way to start receiving structured proposals.
jobs.emptyRoleFreelancer: As a freelancer, browse profiles to understand active services while waiting for new briefs.
jobs.emptyRoleGeneric: If you are hiring, start with one clear brief. If you are freelancing, use the directory to prep your positioning.
jobs.emptyUseCaseOne: Landing page design for a local business website
jobs.emptyUseCaseTwo: Video editing for weekly social media content
jobs.emptyUseCaseThree: On-site event photographer for a weekend event
jobs.emptyUseCaseFour: Math tutor for hybrid online and in-person sessions
jobs.emptyCategoryWhat: What: nothing in this skill bucket right now.
jobs.emptyCategoryWhy: Why: clients only appear when they post real roles—there is no filler data.
jobs.emptyCategoryNext: Next: widen the category or post a brief if you are hiring.
jobs.emptyFiltersWhat: What: zero rows after applying filters.
jobs.emptyFiltersWhy: Why: early boards are sparse and strict filters compound that.
jobs.emptyFiltersNext: Next: loosen one filter at a time, or save jobs you care about.
jobs.emptyActionWhat: What: no open jobs visible on the board.
jobs.emptyActionWhy: Why: early access means postings appear only when clients publish—no phantom listings.
jobs.emptyActionNext: Next: post a credible brief first, then keep proposals and Messages tied to that job.

freelancers.pageTitle: Find and hire freelancers with confidence
freelancers.pageDescription: Compare active freelancers by specialty, location, and work mode, then open profiles to start discussion quickly.
freelancers.exampleHook: Short value statement appears here so clients can compare quickly.
freelancers.exampleMeta: Rating, reviews, location, and work mode are shown in each real listing.
freelancers.trustOne: Profiles, proposals, and chats stay tied to each job.
freelancers.trustTwo: Nearby and remote filters help narrow options faster.
freelancers.trustThree: Compare service fit before you open full profile details.
freelancers.categoryHint: Matches anyone with at least one skill in that category.
freelancers.workModeHint: Use on-site/hybrid for local work, remote for async delivery.
freelancers.cityHint: NearWork treats city as a first-class signal for on-site and hybrid work—add yours to discover nearby freelancers.
freelancers.rateGuideBody: Cards show each freelancer's starting hourly rate (or rate on request) so you can compare budget fit before opening profiles.
freelancers.nearbyIdleHint: Optional: allow browser location to prioritize freelancers close to you. City search remains available.
freelancers.nearbyErrorUnsupported: Geolocation is not supported in this browser.
freelancers.nearbyErrorDenied: Location permission denied. You can still search by city.
freelancers.nearbyErrorLookup: Could not determine your location. Try again or use city search.
freelancers.nearbyBannerBody: Within {{radius}} km · closest first. Distance on cards when coordinates exist.
freelancers.cityMatchBody: Profiles mentioning “{{city}}”—pair with work mode to separate remote vs on-site.
freelancers.resultHint: Open a profile to review work fit, trust signals, and the next action.
freelancers.sidebarSimpleBody: Create your profile once so clients can find your services in this directory.
freelancers.valueStatementFallback: Clear scope and response details available on profile.
freelancers.whyAvailableThisWeek: Available this week for quick kickoff.
freelancers.whyProfileReady: Profile is ready for direct review.
freelancers.liveProposalCount: {{count}} proposals received in the last 24 hours
freelancers.smallCtaTitle: Still not finding the right fit?
freelancers.emptyCategoryTitle: No freelancers in this category yet
freelancers.emptyCategoryBody: No results in this category yet. Try related categories or broaden your filters to discover more options.
freelancers.emptyCategoryStep1: Clear category and try a broader keyword.
freelancers.emptyCategoryStep2: Switch work mode to Any if you over-filtered remote vs on-site.
freelancers.emptyCategoryStep3: Check open jobs if you are hiring instead of browsing talent.
freelancers.emptyFiltersBody: These filters are currently too strict. Adjust one or two fields to surface more matching freelancers.
freelancers.emptyFiltersStep1: Remove city or widen radius if you used nearby.
freelancers.emptyFiltersStep2: Try remote-only for roles that do not need someone on-site.
freelancers.emptyFiltersStep3: Shorten the keyword to one strong term.
freelancers.emptyDefaultBody: Start by exploring categories, nearby mode, or remote mode to uncover relevant freelancers faster.
freelancers.emptyDefaultStep1: If you are a freelancer, complete your profile and set availability.
freelancers.emptyDefaultStep2: If you are hiring, post a job so proposals can start.
freelancers.emptyDefaultStep3: Come back after early-access invites roll out—this list will move first.
freelancers.emptyCategoryWhat: What: nobody is surfaced in this category slice.
freelancers.emptyCategoryWhy: Why: either supply is genuinely low or filters are narrowly stacked.
freelancers.emptyCategoryNext: Next: clear one filter at a time, or pivot to Jobs if you meant to hire for a brief.
freelancers.emptyFiltersWhat: What: no freelancers match everything you selected.
freelancers.emptyFiltersWhy: Why: combined keyword, city, and mode can silently zero out sparse boards.
freelancers.emptyFiltersNext: Next: try remote-first, widen city, or keep one filter at a time.
freelancers.emptyDefaultWhat: What: the roster is genuinely empty.
freelancers.emptyDefaultWhy: Why: freelancers appear only after they publish real profiles—we do not preload fake listings.
freelancers.emptyDefaultNext: Next: create your freelancer profile / post a hiring brief—and keep chats tied to concrete jobs.

freelancerProfile.ctaDescription: If this profile fits your project, start discussion and keep everything tied to this hiring decision.
freelancerProfile.ctaReassurance: You can discuss scope, timeline, and terms before committing.
freelancerProfile.ctaPreview: You will start discussion with this freelancer first - no payment yet.
freelancerProfile.reasonNearbyFit: Good fit for nearby projects in {{city}}.
freelancerProfile.reasonAvailableNow: Available now for new client briefs.
freelancerProfile.reasonStartingRate: Starting rate aligns with shortlisting at {{rate}}.
freelancerProfile.reasonCompleteProfile: Profile is detailed enough for confident comparison.
freelancerProfile.reasonFallback: Profile has enough core details to continue evaluation.
freelancerProfile.aboutFallback: No work overview has been added yet.
freelancerProfile.reviewNoComment: Rating submitted without detailed written feedback.
freelancerProfile.finalCtaBody: Use the contact action to discuss scope, budget, and availability directly.
freelancerProfile.servicesSubtitle: Skills grouped how they appear on NearWork—it reflects what this freelancer emphasizes.
freelancerProfile.portfolioSubtitle: Pieces shared on this profile (opens in a new tab).
freelancerProfile.snapshotCompletedHint: Finished contracts attributed to this account.

jobDetail.skillsEmpty: No skills tagged on this brief yet.
jobDetail.applyDescription: If this brief fits your skills, send a proposal to start discussion tied directly to this job.
jobDetail.applyReassurance: You can send a proposal and discuss details first. No commitment until both sides agree.
jobDetail.formGuideSubtitle: Keep it brief and job-specific so the client can review quickly.
jobDetail.formGuidanceIntro: Mirror the brief and make it easy for clients to compare you in one pass.
jobDetail.formGuidanceBullet1: Intro in your own voice tied to this posting—not a generic flyer.
jobDetail.formGuidanceBullet2: Proof you have shipped similar outcomes before (tools, domains, results).
jobDetail.formGuidanceBullet3: A concrete plan referencing their constraints and unknowns.
jobDetail.formGuidanceBullet4: Honest timeline plus your availability to start or meet milestones.
jobDetail.formGuidanceBullet5: A quote that fits the budget cues and states what is included.
jobDetail.formGuidanceBullet6: A clear call-to-action: invite questions and keep chat on this job listing.
jobDetail.formIntroHint: 1–3 sentences: who you are for this role—skip marketing filler.
jobDetail.formIntroPlaceholder: Introduce yourself in 1–2 lines and nod to the posting you are answering.
jobDetail.formExperienceHint: Mention specific projects, timelines, or stacks that map to this brief.
jobDetail.formExperiencePlaceholder: List 2–3 proof points (client type, deliverable, timeframe) that de-risk the hire.
jobDetail.formApproachHint: Show you read the brief—call out assumptions or questions you already resolved.
jobDetail.formApproachPlaceholder: Outline how you’ll execute, what you need from the client, and your first milestone.
jobDetail.formTimelineHint: Use working days. Note meetings, time zones, or onsite days if relevant.
jobDetail.formTimelinePlaceholder: When you can start, key checkpoints, and realistic delivery range for the scope you priced.
jobDetail.formAmountHint: Use the listing currency. Mention if it covers taxes or optional add-ons.
jobDetail.formDaysHint: Calendar or working days estimate for the scoped work—note if rolling.
jobDetail.formFirstStep: Submitting starts a structured review—finalize details inside the job-linked thread.
jobDetail.formSubmitCtaSubtitle: You can refine scope after the client replies; keep everything anchored to this job.
jobDetail.proposalSent: Proposal sent. You can continue the discussion when the client replies.
jobDetail.proposalError: Could not send proposal. Please check your inputs and try again.
jobDetail.proposalNetworkError: Could not reach the server. Check your connection and try again.
jobDetail.afterProposalHint: Proposal sent and discussion channel is ready.
jobDetail.conversationUnavailableHint: Proposal sent. Open Messages later if this conversation link is not ready yet.
jobDetail.ownerHint: Shortlist to compare later-hire when you are confident every scope detail is clear.
jobDetail.freelancerHint: Send a proposal with price and timing-clients compare you in context with the brief.
jobDetail.ownerAcceptedDesc: Hiring decision is complete. Continue with contract and coordination for delivery.
jobDetail.ownerCompareDesc: Compare freelancers by price, profile readiness, and location signals. Start with pending decisions.
jobDetail.ownerActionSummary: New proposal activity is waiting. Review proposals, start conversation, then shortlist or accept the best fit.
jobDetail.freelancerActionDesc: Sending a proposal requires a signed-in freelancer account. Browse this page freely; sign in or register when you are ready.
jobDetail.pendingDecisionHint: Shortlist to compare, accept when ready
jobDetail.noProposalsYet: No proposals yet. Keep this job open or refine the brief so freelancers can respond with confidence.
jobDetail.jobPostedBannerBody: Proposals arrive here first. Jump to your dashboard if you manage multiple postings.
jobDetail.proposalsEmptyWhy: What: freelancers haven’t responded to this posting. Why: the board might be sparse or brief clarity could improve.
jobDetail.proposalsEmptyNext: Next: sharpen budget & deliverables on the listing, invite saved freelancers, then watch this table for newcomers.
jobDetail.proposalCompletenessOk: Enough to shortlist—ask one clarifying thread if unsure.
jobDetail.discussInviteDetail: Kick off Messages to clarify scope—it stays tethered to this job.
jobDetail.threadReadyDetail: Thread opened—send a succinct note tied to their proposal.
jobDetail.discussProminentHint: Start or resume Messages to align scope, budget, or timing—all tied back to this job.
jobDetail.reportSecondaryNote: Use report only for trust issues—your hiring buttons stay above.
jobDetail.bidHintShortlisted: Ready to hire when you're confident
jobDetail.signInToSend: Sign in to send a proposal
jobDetail.originalLanguageOnly: Translated copy is not available yet; showing original text.

forbidden.title: Access denied
forbidden.description: You do not have permission to open this area of the app. Use the dashboard for your account type, or sign in with a different user.

nearbySearch.title: Nearby search
nearbySearch.description: Combine geolocation inputs with your GeoService and search APIs.
nearbySearch.mapDescription: Map UI reads query state from URL or form - logic stays in hooks/services.
nearbySearch.resultsDescription: Render freelancers or jobs returned from the search layer.

moderation.dialogLead: Describe what violates our community guidelines. Staff reviews every report.
moderation.descriptionHint: Include enough detail for staff to verify (minimum 10 characters).
moderation.success: Report received. Our team will review it.
moderation.error: Could not submit the report. Try again.
moderation.onboardingJobsStep1: Post a brief that states scope, deadline, and budget.
moderation.onboardingJobsStep3: Reply in Messages to clarify scope before you hire.
moderation.onboardingProposalStep1: Complete your profile so clients recognize your credibility.
moderation.onboardingProposalStep2: Send focused proposals tailored to open jobs.
moderation.onboardingProposalStep3: Watch for replies—timely messages close more wins.

earlyAccess.description: We are shipping in public: real jobs, real freelancers, and honest limits—while we finish payments and edge cases.
earlyAccess.whyBody: A marketplace only works if briefs and bids feel trustworthy. We would rather grow with a small, vocal community than fake scale.
earlyAccess.todayItem1: Browse open jobs and freelancer profiles
earlyAccess.todayItem2: Post jobs and collect bids (as a client)
earlyAccess.todayItem3: Lean on city and work mode when nearby work matters
earlyAccess.evolvingPrefix: Payouts, dispute tooling, and some polish paths are not finished. If something breaks, use

pricing.descriptionSuffix: We are not selling you a plan today—we want real jobs, real profiles, and feedback while the product settles. Here is how money works right now (and what might change later).
pricing.currentlyFreeBody1: Using NearWork during early access does not require a paid subscription. You can browse public jobs and freelancer profiles, create an account, and use the core flows we have shipped without hitting a pricing gate.
pricing.currentlyFreeBold: No subscription is required right now.
pricing.currentlyFreeBody2: If we ever ask for payment for platform access, we will say so clearly here and in-product first—not through hidden charges on work you already started.
pricing.donationBody: If you want to help cover costs while we build, you may see an optional way to donate inside the product. It is entirely voluntary: skipping it does not limit your profile, your jobs, or how you show up in search.
pricing.futureBody1: We may introduce paid options down the road—things like boosted visibility, team features, or other add-ons. Nothing is finalized; we will not publish fake price tags or countdowns to pressure you.
pricing.futureBody2: When paid tiers exist, this page will list what you get and what it costs before we turn anything on for new charges.
pricing.ctaBody: You do not need a price sheet to try NearWork—look around, then sign up if it fits.

help.description: NearWork is a small product in early access. We are not a 24/7 support desk yet—this page is a honest anchor for how to reach us and what we can help with.
help.contactBody: For questions, bugs, or trust and safety reports, email us at the address below. We read everything; response time depends on volume while the team is small.
help.contactNote: Placeholder—replace with your real support inbox before you point customers here.
help.topicsBody: Full articles can live here later; for now these are the buckets we expect to document.
help.topic1Body: Creating an account, roles (client vs freelancer), and what you can do in early access.
help.topic2Body: Posting a brief, reviewing proposals, and what happens after you pick someone.
help.topic3Body: City, work mode, and how you show up to clients searching nearby or remote.
help.topic4Body: Placeholder topic—full guidance will land when checkout and payouts are wired end to end.
help.topic5Body: Errors, abuse, or scam patterns—reach out with what you saw and when; screenshots help.
help.selfServeItem2Suffix: what works today vs what is still moving
help.selfServeItem3Suffix: free during early access; donations optional

how.description: NearWork connects clients and freelancers around briefs, proposals, and delivery—with your city and work mode in the loop when place matters. The product is real; the roadmap is still open.
how.clientsSubtitle: When you need talent for a defined piece of work.
how.clientsStep1Body: Describe the outcome, budget, and timeline. Say whether you need someone nearby, remote, or hybrid—clients use location fields when the work is not only online.
how.clientsStep2Body: Freelancers respond with proposals on your job. You compare scope, price, and timing in one place instead of scattered threads.
how.clientsStep3Body: Choose who fits, align on milestones, and keep decisions next to the work. The details depend on your project—NearWork is the shared starting line, not a replacement for how you run delivery day to day.
how.freelancersSubtitle: When you want briefs that respect how and where you work.
how.freelancersStep1Body: Add skills, work mode, and where you can show up in person. That helps clients who care about nearby talent actually discover you—not just your headline.
how.freelancersStep2Body: Use the public job board and filters (including city and mode) to find briefs that match how you work. Listings are live data from the marketplace—not a demo feed.
how.freelancersStep3Body: Reply with clear scope, price, and timing. A strong proposal is specific to the brief—generic pitches do not help you or the client.
how.nearbyBody: Nearby fits shoots, installs, on-site support, and anything that needs someone in a place. Remote fits work that ships online. Hybrid sits in between. You pick the mode per job or per profile so expectations stay explicit.
how.earlyAccessBody: NearWork is still in early access: features and coverage will grow, and some flows will change as we learn from real usage. We are not pretending the marketplace is complete—we are inviting people who are okay building with us.
how.earlyAccessLink: Read what early access means today →
how.ctaBody: Create an account to post or bid, or explore the public board first—no hype, just the same product everyone else sees.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.subtitle: Draft placeholder for early access. Replace with counsel-reviewed terms before charging money at scale.
terms.body1: By using NearWork during early access you agree to use the product responsibly: no fraudulent listings, no harassment, and no attempts to bypass platform safety controls we add over time.
terms.body2: Payments and dispute resolution may rely on third-party providers; final legal language will describe those relationships clearly.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.subtitle: Draft placeholder for early access. Replace with counsel-reviewed text before production marketing.
privacy.body1: NearWork processes account data (email, name, role) and content you post (jobs, bids, messages) to run the marketplace. We use cookies for signed-in sessions. We do not sell personal data to third parties in this early-access build.
